CONTENTS

CHAPTER I. The Beginning
CHAPTER II. The Middle
CHAPTER III. The End

CHAPTER I

The morning broke grey and slow over the harbour town, and the gulls cried
above the masts as the tide came in. He walked the length of the quay with his
hands buried deep in his coat, turning over in his mind the long road that had
brought him to this cold northern place at last. Every shopfront was shuttered
and every window dark, and the only light was the lamp at the customs house.

CHAPTER II

By noon the streets had filled again with carts and criers and the smell of
fish, and the whole town seemed to have shaken off the chill of the dawn. She
met him at the corner by the chandler's and they spoke of small things, of the
weather and the price of rope, while underneath the talk ran a deeper current
neither of them was willing yet to name aloud in the bright and busy street.

CHAPTER III

When the evening came the lamps were lit one by one along the front, and the
sea turned the colour of slate and then of ink. They stood together at the rail
and watched the last boat come in, and knew that whatever had been decided
between them in the silence of the afternoon could not now be undone, and that
the road ahead, whatever it held, they would walk it from here on together.
